title: nepeta bombaiensis
intro: Nepeta bombaiensis, also known as Indian catmint, is a perennial plant native to the Himalayas in India and other parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Nepeta bombaiensis include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as a natural antihistamine, making it effective in treating allergies and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, Nepeta bombaiensis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical applications such as creams and ointments.
